title: symphoricarpos orbiculatus var. spicatus
intro: Symphoricarpos orbiculatus var. spicatus, also known as the coralberry bush, is a shrub native to North America with vibrant red berries and white or pink flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The health benefits of the coralberry bush include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the coralberry bush may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used to create topical creams and salves for wound healing and skin care.
